name: reactivated

metadata.name: reactivated
metadata.summary: A statically typed framework to create Django sites with a React frontend.

documentation:
# Zero-configuration Django and React. Together at last.

Reactivated is the easiest way to use Django and React together.

You get the full power of Django. Rendered by React

No webpack, no config, no tooling. Just React and Django.

https://www.reactivated.io

modules.name: sample.server.urls
documentation:
server URL Configuration

The `urlpatterns` list routes URLs to views. For more information please see:
    https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.2/topics/http/urls/
Examples:
Function views
    1. Add an import:  from my_app import views
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', views.home, name='home')
Class-based views
    1. Add an import:  from other_app.views import Home
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', Home.as_view(), name='home')
Including another URLconf
    1. Import the include() function: from django.urls import include, path
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('blog/', include('blog.urls'))

functions.name: reactivated.utils.is_simple_callable
documentation: Mock is_simple_callable for now
instead of the more sophisticated one from rest_framework.fields

functions.name: reactivated.utils.get_attribute
documentation:
Similar to Python's built in `getattr(instance, attr)`,
but takes a list of nested attributes, instead of a single attribute.
Also accepts either attribute lookup on objects or dictionary lookups.
From Django Rest Framework 3.6

classes.name: reactivated.utils.ClassLookupDict
documentation:
Takes a dictionary with classes as keys.
Lookups against this object will traverses the object's inheritance
hierarchy in method resolution order, and returns the first matching value
from the dictionary or raises a KeyError if nothing matches.
From https://github.com/encode/django-rest-framework/blob/master/rest_framework/utils/field_mapping.py

methods.name: reactivated.constraints.EnumConstraint.constraint_sql
documentation:
We leave this blank as the migration code tries to inject
constraint code inline with the field, which doesn't work for custom
types in PostgreSQL.

methods.name: reactivated.fields.EnumChoice.__hash__
documentation: Used by DRF when creating choices in `rest_framework/fields.py`

classes.name: reactivated.fields.EnumChoiceIterator
documentation:
This is a special iterator that preserves the original enum. Useful so
we can use the "choices" argument that triggers special Django behaviors,
but leave our enum intact for reference.

methods.name: reactivated.fields._EnumField.contribute_to_class
documentation:
We don't store the enum in the constraint. Instead, we store the fields
so the autodetection for changed enums works automatically.

functions.name: reactivated.apps.get_types_schema
documentation:
The package json-schema-to-typescript does expose a way to
automatically export any interface it sees. However, this can bloat our
generated files.

Instead, while creating the schema, we occasionally run into types that we
want available globally but are not directly referenced by templates.

These aren't exported by `json-schem-to-typescript` because they're
referenced using `tsType`, so the libraary is unaware of their usage.

So we register them in `globals` and force `json-schema-to-typescript` to
expose them.

We can't just add these types to the `type_registry` because that's only
parsed once when generating the parent tuple.

We could explore doing two passes in the future.

See `unreachableDefinitions` in json-schema-to-typescript

methods.name: reactivated.apps.ReactivatedConfig.ready
documentation:
Django's dev server actually starts twice. So we prevent generation on
the first start. TODO: handle noreload.

methods.name: reactivated.forms.EnumChoiceField.__init__
documentation:
When instantiated by a model form, choices will be populated and
enum will not, as Django strips all but a defined set of kwargs.

And coerce will be populated by the model as well.

When using this field directly in a form, enum will be populated and
choices and coerce should be None.

inside an installed application.
app_dirname = 'foobar'

functions.name: reactivated.terminate_proc
documentation:
npm exec doesn't correctly forward signals to its child processes. So,
simply calling proc.terminate() doesn't actually kill the process. Rather,
we have to send SIGTERM to the entire process group.
Note: using this requires that the initial call to subprocess.Popen included
the `start_new_session=True` flag.

functions.name: reactivated.extract_views_from_urlpatterns
documentation:
Heavily modified version of the functiuon from django_extensions/management/commands/show_urls.py

Return a list of views from a list of urlpatterns.

Each object in the returned list is a three-tuple: (view_func, regex, name, pattern)

Given the incoming request, return a tokenized list of media

From https://github.com/encode/django-rest-framework/blob/master/rest_framework/negotiation.py

classes.name: reactivated.templates.LazySerializationResponse

methods.name: reactivated.templates.LazySerializationResponse.__getstate__
documentation:
First run the normal pickling of `TemplateResponse`. That already has
special handling.

Then, remove our template from pickling because it cannot and should
not be pickled. The response only cares about the rendered content.

This lazy serialization doesn't actually convert our context to JSON
until the very last minute, using resolve_context.

That allows things like our autocomplete decorator and other functions
that peek into the context to behave as normal. They'll be handed
regular Python objects.

functions.name: reactivated.serialization.extract_widget_context
documentation:
Previously we used a custom FormRenderer but there is *no way* to avoid
the built in render method from piping this into `mark_safe`.

So we monkeypatch the widget's internal renderer to return JSON directly
without being wrapped by `mark_safe`.

methods.name: reactivated.serialization.FieldType.get_json_schema
documentation: TODO: way to mark this as a custom property we define. This is just so it is
marked as required.
The actual widget name is done by `form_schema`, which is kind of odd.
We need a better way to make a custom schema that is self contained.
widget: Any

classes.name: reactivated.serialization.UnionType
documentation: if TYPE_CHECKING is False:
try:
import django_extensions.db.fields  # type: ignore[import]
register(django_extensions.db.fields.ShortUUIDField)(str)
except ImportError:
pass
def field_descriptor_schema(
Type: "models.Field[Any, Any]", definitions: Definitions
) -> Thing:
FieldSchemaWithPossibleNull = stubs._GenericAlias(origin=Union, params=(Type, None) if Type.null is True else (Type,))
return create_schema(FieldSchemaWithPossibleNull, definitions)
